one_line_ask: Audit Willowfen Press's supplied 32-page KDP picture-book interior .indd, its exported interior PDF, and the wraparound cover PDF against the KDP spec sheet — checking trim, bleed, margins, spine width, image resolution and caption overset — and emit a marked-up preflight report plus proof renders that says whether the files will pass KDP preflight.
brief: Willowfen Press is a small independent children's-book publisher about to upload its next paperback, "Pip & the Paper Moon", to Amazon KDP. Our illustrator and layout designer have already built the files, but our last title bounced twice in KDP's preflight (spine width off, one illustration under-resolution, a caption clipped), so before we upload again we want an independent print-QA pass. We are handing off four things: (1) the authored InDesign interior template (pip_interior_template.indd) — a 32-page 8.5x11 in trim book with real InDesign data-merge caption fields (<<page_no>>, <<caption>>, <<alt_note>>) placed on each illustrated spread; (2) the exported interior PDF our designer produced from it (pip_interior_export.pdf, 32 pages); (3) the wraparound paperback cover our designer built to the KDP cover template (pip_cover_wrap.pdf — front + spine + back on one sheet); and (4) the KDP spec sheet we were given (kdp_spec_sheet.pdf) that states the required trim (8.5x11 in), bleed (0.125 in on outside edges), interior safe margin (0.375 in), minimum image resolution (300 DPI), and the spine-width formula for a 32-page white-paper paperback (~0.0725 in).
